svg.text: LIBRAS
GRAMMAR — SOUTH AMERICAN SIGN SYNTAX
LIBRAS WORD ORDER
LIBRAS: SOV is most common, but SVO also occurs
PESSOA LIVRO DAR: "Person gives book" (SOV)
Topic-comment very frequent: LIVRO [brows] PESSOA DAR
More flexible than spoken Portuguese (spatial grammar reduces word-order need)
LIBRAS SPATIAL GRAMMAR
Loci: nouns assigned locations in space; pointing = pronoun
Agreement verbs: move between loci to show who acts on whom
Classifiers (proforms): represent categories of referents
Same spatial grammar as ASL, LSF (shared through LSF ancestor)
LIBRAS ASPECT
Aspect over tense: same as all sign languages
Movement modification: repeated = habitual; tense = time sign + context
Timeline: future = space forward • past = space behind (universal SL feature)
LSA GRAMMAR · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
LSA WORD ORDER
LSA: tends toward SOV; topic-comment also common
PERSONA LIBRO DAR: "Person gives book" (SOV like LIBRAS)
Both LIBRAS and LSA: SOV with spatial modification (parallel)
LSA SPATIAL GRAMMAR
LSA: same spatial grammar system as LIBRAS (universal to sign languages)
Loci, verb agreement, classifiers: all present
Argentina research: Universidad Nacional del Comahue; APSA (Deaf association)
GRAMMAR COMPARISON
Both: SOV-preferred • both: topic-comment • both: spatial grammar
Both: aspect over tense • both: classifiers
Vocabulary: largely distinct (different languages)
Grammar: largely parallel (shared sign language universals + LSF heritage)
PLATE 66c ALT · LIBRAS / LSA · GRAMMAR
